findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\]"` hits `Weight.lean:62:@[expose] noncomputable def chiNumerator` and `Weight.lean:88:@[expose] noncomputable def chi`; nearby `Characteristic.lean:79` explicitly says the canonical body is not part of the interface and supplies `canonicalCharacteristic_apply` instead.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from both definitions and rely on `chiNumerator_def`, `chi_def`, `chiNumerator_even`, and `two_mul_chi`; add any missing characteristic lemmas instead of exposing bodies.
findings.issue: `chiNumerator` and `chi` are public definitions marked `@[expose]`, making their bodies part of the automation/unfolding surface without a demonstrated downstream need to compute by unfolding.

summary: The added weight API exposes definition bodies even though the file already provides characteristic lemmas for using them without unfolding.
